subject: Gaming
title: The Mental Health Cost of Indie Dev
description: A fast breakdown of The Mental Health Cost of Indie Dev for builders.

hook.text: Indie success stories hide the burnout. Many solo developers work 80-hour weeks for years with no guarantee.

buildup.text: Without teams, indie devs handle coding, art, music, marketing, and customer support alone.

discovery.text: Studies show indie developers report higher rates of burnout, anxiety, and depression than other tech workers.

twist.text: Even successful devs suffer. Stardew Valley's creator talked openly about the isolation of solo development.

climax.text: The industry is slowly acknowledging this. Support communities and mental health resources are growing.

punchline.text: Behind every dream game is a developer who almost quit. Success costs.

quiz.question: Why do indie developers face high burnout rates?
options: They handle all aspects of development alone with no guaranteed outcome
They work fewer hours than studio developers
Indie games are easier to make
